summary: Credential stealer and remote-code-execution backdoor in @ts-apis/ts-utils
details: On require(), dist/index.js runs a setTimeout-deferred IIFE that exfiltrates developer and CI secrets to safdadfasf.com:443/cb/loopscale/exfil. All sensitive strings (hostnames, paths, shell commands) are hidden via a String.fromCharCode-based decoder (_s) to evade grep scanners. The payload reads ~/.ssh/id_*, ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.kube/config, ~/.npmrc, ~/.netrc, ~/.docker/config.json, ~/.git-credentials, plus gcloud/azure/terraform/solana/vault/railway config files and any.env* in the working directory, and filters process.env for KEY|SECRET|TOKEN|PASS|AWS|... before POSTing to the attacker host. It also harvests cloud instance-metadata credentials: AWS IMDSv2 (PUT /latest/api/token then GET /latest/meta-data/iam/security-credentials/<role>), GCP metadata.google.internal (/computeMetadata/v1/instance/service-accounts/default/token), and AWS ECS task credentials via $AWS_CONTAINER_CREDENTIALS_RELATIVE_URI. A persistent C2 loop polls safdadfasf.com/cb/loopscale/h?id=<host-tag> every 10 seconds; if the response contains a shell string it is passed to execSync, and if it begins with 'SLIVER' the payload downloads an attacker-specified URL, chmods it 755, and spawns it detached via `sh -c <path> &`, providing full RCE and Sliver-style implant delivery. The package advertises itself as generic 'Shared TypeScript utility functions for API services' but the exfil path (/cb/loopscale/) and BRIDGESPLIT_AUTHORITY references indicate targeting of Loopscale/Solana ecosystem developers.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
